And the Lord spake to Noah, and to his sons with him, saying,

KLV: Therefore the Lord himself shall give you a sign; Behold, a virgin shall conceive, and bear a son, and shall call his name Immanuel.

In the Old Testament the birth of the Master was foretold in the Book of Isaiah: “Therefore the Lord himself shall give you a sign; Behold, a virgin shall conceive, and bear a son, and shall call his name Immanuel.” (Isaiah 7:14.) And in the Book of Micah, we are told he would be born in Bethlehem. (Micah 5:2.) (Howard W. Hunter, in Conference Report, Oct. 1968, 140; or Improvement Era, Dec.…

From his knowledge of the scriptures President Roberts had selected and adopted a slogan for the mission. That slogan was just one word, "Immanuel." Isaiah, in foretelling the birth of the Savior, foretold, "Therefore the Lord himself shall give you a sign; Behold, a virgin shall conceive, and bear a son, and shall call his name Immanuel." (Isaiah 7:14.) Centuries later, Matthew, an apostle of…

7:14: "Behold, a virgin shall conceive, and bear a son, and shall call his name Immanuel." This idiom is found in a number of places in the Book of Mormon: (see Alma 50:13-14; Mosiah 1:2; Alma 23:17) (John A. Tvedtnes in Rediscovering the Book of Mormon, 89.)

matches.lb: Quoting Commentary: Ibn Ezra on Isaiah 7:14:1
matches.x: Therefore the Lord himself etc. Though you do not ask a sign, He will give it you. It is to me a matter of surprise that there are those who say the prophet here refers to Jesus, since the sign was given to Ahaz, and Jesus was born many years afterwards; besides, the prophet says, For before the child shall know to refuse the evil and choose the good, the land shall be forsaken; but the countries…
KJV: Butter and honey shall he eat, that he may know to refuse the evil, and choose the good.

2 But as for all them that reject ... when God will bring a visitation upon the land they shall be requited with the recompense of the wicked ; when there shall come to pass the word which is written in the words of Isaiah the son of Amos 3 the prophet, who said : ‘ He will bring upon thee and upon thy people and upon thy father’s house days that have (not) come from the day that Ephraim departed…

KJV: Butter and honey shall he eat, that he may know to refuse the evil, and choose the good.

Why is eating butter and honey related to refusing evil? This sign was given to the Old Testament King Ahaz, encouraging him to take his strength from the Lord rather than the military might of Damascus, Samaria, or other militant camps. Ahaz was slow to hear that counsel, but the Lord gave it anyway (Jeffrey R. Holland, Christ and the New Covenant: The Messianic Message of the Book of Mormon,…
